title: fraxinus excelsior f. leucocarpa
intro: Fraxinus excelsior f. leucocarpa, also known as the ash-leaved or white-fruited ash, is a subspecies of the European ash tree (Fraxinus excelsior). The bark, leaves, and seeds of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds like tannins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus excelsior f. leucocarpa are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Fraxinus excelsior f. leucocarpa is prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
